what_it_does: It is a staged build plan for integrating an existing fabric AI-member mesh with OpenWebUI without forking it, using OpenWebUI channel webhooks for identity-preserving message mirroring and a thin OpenAI-protocol adapter for supervisor chat.
observations: OpenWebUI 0.9.6 is already running and verified via Tailscale PWA on Tim's phone.
The channels webhook primitive is verified by-use: admin create + token-only post yields a live message with member identity.
The fabric brain_router and fleet source already exist, so 'chat with the supervisor' is mostly an adapter problem.
Integration is split into four additive stages: the room, the concierge, Tim's voice back, and hygiene.
Each fabric member self-registers by causing creation of a dedicated webhook carrying its name/avatar.
The plan explicitly avoids forking OpenWebUI; LibreChat is kept only as a documented fallback.
Propose-floor is preserved: the phone surface reads and proposes but cannot spawn or inject.
standouts: Per-member webhook identity is the central transport mechanism for mirroring the fabric mesh into OpenWebUI.
The only identified real gap for supervisor chat is a thin OpenAI-protocol adapter over brain_router.ask.
Stages are additive and deliberately non-invasive to OpenWebUI.
conventions: Uses a status header and separates 'already real/verified' from 'planned'.
Cites prior research reports (01..08 + 04b) and internal decisions (r9) inline.
Stages are numbered and additive, with a fallback section at the end.
Security and control boundaries are called out as cross-cutting decisions.
concerns: The bridge endpoint POST /api/channel/post is described as 'ungated, live', which raises authentication/authorization questions not detailed here.
The fabric must hold an OpenWebUI admin token to create webhooks, but rotation and storage details are not specified.
One webhook per member per channel scales linearly and could become unwieldy.
Pinning to OpenWebUI 0.9.6 may block future security or feature updates.
notes: This is the agent-registered build doc that turns a verified OpenWebUI webhook smoke test into a four-stage, non-fork integration plan for Tim to watch and join the fabric mesh from his phone.
questions: What is the exact schema for storing webhook_id and token on a member's channel registration?
How is the 'ungated' bridge endpoint POST /api/channel/post secured or rate-limited?
What does 'read+propose floor' mean in terms of concrete API permissions or code paths?
How will the OpenWebUI admin token be rotated and stored securely by the fabric?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the file presents itself as a staged, post-research build plan and does exactly that.
apparent_themes: Identity-preserving transport between autonomous-agent mesh and human UI
intention_signals: It is trying to give Tim a secure, phone-accessible window into the fabric AI mesh by reusing OpenWebUI's existing channel/webhook substrate and a minimal adapter, while keeping the fabric's lead-only control boundaries intact.
novelty: It treats OpenWebUI channel webhooks as a per-member, identity-carrying bus for an external multi-agent mesh, and recasts 'chat with the supervisor' as a standard OpenAI-compatible model connection rather than a custom UI feature.
contribution: A staged decomposition that separates visibility, supervisor chat, bidirectional participation, and cognitive hygiene; the webhook-per-member identity mapping; the OpenAI-adapter pattern for supervisor access; the explicit no-fork/additive constraint; and a documented fallback if the substrate fails.
summary_for_embedding: A staged build plan for integrating an existing fabric AI-member mesh with OpenWebUI 0.9.6 using per-member channel webhooks and a thin OpenAI-protocol adapter, enabling a human observer to watch and participate in multi-agent conversations from a phone without forking OpenWebUI.
intention_for_embedding: To record the verified, additive integration path from the fabric mesh to OpenWebUI so the team can implement the room, concierge, reply-path, and hygiene stages in order.
